skill.name: interaction-design
skill.description: Design and implement UI microinteractions, motion patterns, loading states, transitions, gestures, and feedback behaviors for modern web interfaces.
skill.summary: Add purposeful motion and feedback patterns to product interfaces.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found markdown examples, TypeScript snippets, and ordinary design terms. No prompt injection, credential access, executable installer, or malicious data exfiltration was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill includes browser fetch and navigation examples that users should adapt carefully.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are false positives from fenced TSX and CSS examples. They are documentation snippets, not executed shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown code fences and frontend examples. I found no script file, install hook, shell interpreter, or user input command path.

low_findings.title: Browser Fetch and Documentation Links Require Normal Review
low_findings.description: The network detections are a browser View Transitions example and links to public documentation. No secrets or local data are sent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch call uses a function argument in an illustrative frontend snippet. The hardcoded URLs are resource links, not hidden callbacks or exfiltration endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words or animation examples. No cryptographic API is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows design prose, comments, and animation cleanup logic. There is no DES, MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are UI State Examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are false positives from browser APIs and timestamp identifiers used in animation and notification examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited snippets use Date.now, matchMedia, and scroll event timing for UI behavior. They do not enumerate files, users, processes, or host configuration.

content.value_statement: Static interfaces can feel unclear when feedback and transitions are missing. This skill guides motion, loading states, gestures, and feedback patterns for product UIs.
actual_capabilities: Provides timing guidelines, easing functions, and motion principles for UI interactions.
Shows patterns for loading states, skeleton screens, progress indicators, and button feedback.
Covers Framer Motion, GSAP, Web Animations API, and CSS animation approaches.
Includes examples for page transitions, gestures, toasts, dialogs, and navigation indicators.
Documents scroll animations, parallax effects, reveal patterns, and reduced motion support.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete production design system.
Generated code still needs testing in the target framework and browser support matrix.
The examples assume common React and frontend animation libraries.
It does not replace accessibility review for motion sensitivity or keyboard behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Feedback
use_cases.description: Add loading, success, error, and confirmation states that make product actions easier to understand.

use_cases.title: Implement Frontend Motion
use_cases.description: Build practical React motion patterns with Framer Motion, CSS transitions, GSAP, or browser animation APIs.

use_cases.title: Review Interaction Quality
use_cases.description: Audit an interface for excessive motion, missing feedback, poor timing, and reduced motion gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Button Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small interaction improvement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add clear hover, press, loading, success, and error feedback to this button. Keep the motion accessible and brief.

prompt_templates.title: Design Loading States
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a common product workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create loading, empty, error, and success states for this dashboard view. Use skeletons and progress cues where useful.

prompt_templates.title: Build Page Transitions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for route and component transitions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design page and modal transitions for this React app. Include timing, easing, interruption behavior, and reduced motion handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Motion System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for systematic interaction review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this interface motion system. Identify missing feedback, excessive animation, performance risks, and accessibility fixes.

output_examples.input: A save button feels unresponsive after users click it.
output_examples.output: A recommended interaction plan with pressed feedback, a loading label, disabled repeat clicks, success confirmation, and error recovery.

output_examples.input: A page transition feels abrupt between dashboard routes.
output_examples.output: A motion specification with enter and exit timing, easing choices, layout continuity, and reduced motion behavior.

output_examples.input: A long marketing page needs scroll-triggered reveals.
output_examples.output: A staged reveal approach that uses intersection observation, transform-based animation, performance limits, and accessibility safeguards.

best_practices: Use motion to clarify feedback, orientation, focus, or continuity.
Keep common interactions fast and support reduced motion preferences.
Animate transform and opacity when performance matters.
anti_patterns: Do not add decorative motion that delays task completion.
Do not block input while nonessential animation plays.
Do not animate layout-heavy properties when transform alternatives work.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create microinteractions, loading states, transitions, gestures, feedback patterns, and scroll animations for web interfaces.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It provides strong implementation patterns, but teams should test the result in their app and browser targets.

faq.question: Which animation tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Framer Motion, GSAP, CSS transitions, CSS keyframes, Web Animations API, and native View Transitions.

faq.question: Is accessibility included?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights reduced motion support, clear feedback, interruptible animation, and progressive enhancement.

faq.question: Can it help with performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends transform and opacity animation, careful will-change use, and cleanup of listeners or animations.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Frontend engineers, product designers, and UX teams can use it to improve interaction quality in product interfaces.
